Rock Paper Scissors Image Recognition¶

Vishal Bakshi
08/04/2020

In [26]:
%reload_ext autoreload
%autoreload 2
%matplotlib inline

from fastai.vision import *
from fastai.metrics import error_rate

bs = 64

Set Path to Images¶

In [27]:
path = Path("images/rps")
path_rock_img = Path("images/rps/rock")
path_scissors_img = Path("images/rps/scissors")
path_paper_img = Path("images/rps/paper")

fnames = get_image_files(path_scissors_img)
fnames[:5]
Out[27]:
[PosixPath('images/rps/scissors/0657zSfiTYzP2jrl.png'),
 PosixPath('images/rps/scissors/0CSaM2vL2cWX6Cay.png'),
 PosixPath('images/rps/scissors/0ePX1wuCc3et7leL.png'),
 PosixPath('images/rps/scissors/0Flw60Z2MAWWKn6S.png'),
 PosixPath('images/rps/scissors/0Ug54ifXRqqlZS2Z.png')]

Create ImageDataBunch¶

In [28]:
np.random.seed(42)
data = ImageDataBunch.from_folder(path, train=".", valid_pct=0.2,
        ds_tfms=get_transforms(), size=224, num_workers=4).normalize(imagenet_stats)
In [29]:
data.classes, data.c, len(data.train_ds), len(data.valid_ds)
Out[29]:
(['paper', 'rock', 'scissors'], 3, 1751, 437)
In [30]:
data.show_batch(rows=3, figsize=(7,6))

Training: resnet34¶

In [31]:
learn = cnn_learner(data, models.resnet34, metrics=error_rate)
In [33]:
learn.fit_one_cycle(4)
epoch train_loss valid_loss error_rate time
0 0.621202 0.045597 0.009153 00:09
1 0.282762 0.020757 0.009153 00:08
2 0.167352 0.016648 0.006865 00:08
3 0.115339 0.014631 0.004577 00:07
In [34]:
learn.save('stage-1')
In [35]:
interp = ClassificationInterpretation.from_learner(learn)
losses,idxs = interp.top_losses()
len(data.valid_ds)==len(losses)==len(idxs)
Out[35]:
True
In [36]:
interp.plot_top_losses(9, figsize=(15,11))
In [37]:
interp.plot_confusion_matrix(dpi=120)
In [39]:
interp.most_confused(min_val=1)
Out[39]:
[('paper', 'scissors', 1), ('rock', 'paper', 1)]

Fine-Tuning¶

In [40]:
learn.unfreeze()
learn.fit_one_cycle(1)
epoch train_loss valid_loss error_rate time
0 0.137574 0.105214 0.016018 00:08
In [41]:
learn.load('stage-1');
In [56]:
learn.lr_find()
50.00% [2/4 00:14<00:14]
epoch train_loss valid_loss error_rate time
0 0.042965 #na# 00:07
1 0.044012 #na# 00:07

25.93% [7/27 00:02<00:07 0.1179] 
LR Finder is complete, type {learner_name}.recorder.plot() to see the graph.
In [57]:
learn.recorder.plot()
In [65]:
learn.lr_find(start_lr = 1e-20)
75.00% [3/4 00:22<00:07]
epoch train_loss valid_loss error_rate time
0 0.058673 #na# 00:07
1 0.057329 #na# 00:07
2 0.065532 #na# 00:07

22.22% [6/27 00:02<00:08 0.1229] 
LR Finder is complete, type {learner_name}.recorder.plot() to see the graph.
In [66]:
learn.recorder.plot()
In [67]:
learn.lr_find()
learn.recorder.plot()
50.00% [2/4 00:14<00:14]
epoch train_loss valid_loss error_rate time
0 0.065354 #na# 00:07
1 0.049949 #na# 00:07

33.33% [9/27 00:03<00:06 0.1710] 
LR Finder is complete, type {learner_name}.recorder.plot() to see the graph.
In [69]:
learn.unfreeze()
learn.fit_one_cycle(4, max_lr=slice(1e-6,1e-3))
epoch train_loss valid_loss error_rate time
0 0.032108 0.006666 0.004577 00:08
1 0.027973 0.010578 0.006865 00:08
2 0.022365 0.002613 0.000000 00:08
3 0.017293 0.002218 0.000000 00:08
In [70]:
learn.save('stage-2');
In [71]:
interp = ClassificationInterpretation.from_learner(learn)
losses,idxs = interp.top_losses()
len(data.valid_ds)==len(losses)==len(idxs)
Out[71]:
True
In [72]:
interp.plot_top_losses(9, figsize=(15,11))
In [73]:
interp.plot_confusion_matrix(dpi=120)

100% accuracy. That's insane. And probably overfitted. To be continued...
